item.title: How to troll neo-Nazis
item.description: Today in the German city of Osnabrück around 1,500 protesters peacefully surrounded a promotion stand of the neo-Nazi party AfD. The people at the stand could get out, but nobody could reach the stand. Trolling and finger pointing out Nazis seems to become a rapidly growing trend in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Che07

item.dc:creator: Copperbrat

item.title: Ah yep, this feels like a Mjonday again. You can tell by all the smoke. Here's 271nd album as a treat.
item.description:
Woo. I'm back. It was a holiday. I tend to miss holiday monjdays, don't know why. No eromeme OC here. Just things I've pulled from the bonfire.
Qvuack.
Just say hey, I wanna do the thing.
Where to begin? Boys, girls, and everything in between, I'm tired. I wish I could say it's normal and natural, but it isn't. It's hate, spite, and petty bullshit. It's ignorance and apathy, and it's feeling like the only way out it violence against people who want to debate some people's *right to exist*. But I can't condone that. So, instead, help me by being kind. Help those in need so we can stand together. Be patient, be understanding. Nurture love and life, and hobbies, and learning. Be a safe space for your friends whose lives and livelihoods are under attack. This country is kinda fucky right now but I saw a quote from like...2016? That is just as important as its ever gonna be: "I'm going to wake up and try to do good and so are you and nobody gets to vote on that." I know this message is coming from a place of turmoil. And it's easy to sit behind our phones and throw out our hate and cynicism about who is responsible for what. So today, I'm going to ask a favor. If you want to directly contribute to this topic, don't add to the hate. Tell us about someone you know who has acted with kindness and compassion for the sake of being kind (if you think they wouldn't mind). They may not brag about it, but we can all stand to see it exists. Stay safe, everyone. Anyway, heres the archive. May your way be Green and Golden. https://imgur.com/a/vhx2cjG
item.dc:creator: Icouri

item.title: Hilarious Monday Meme Dump Generated By Humans #621
item.description:
1. Expectations
2. The After Credit Sceen: OOOPS
3. Eastern European Female Mating Dance (Would you "fall" for it?)
4. The cop's face
5. It's Gym, what?
6. Warning a notorious thief spotted in the neighborhood
7
8. "What are they gonna do? Arrest me?"
9
10. I will let you write a funny caption for this one, you've been on Imgur long enough
11
12
13
14. This shovel has interesting design...
15. If you wait long enough this Goose appears in the Main Menu! For context: I make indie games and currently working on a wacky one called Do Not Press The Button and it's fun to make but boy do I struggle at times and there is some amount of jank ( that I hope players will find endearing). I didn't have much expectations, just a fun little idea I had a reverse psychology game. The demo actually was received well and from then things started to pick up and now we're here : ) If you decide to support us with a wishlist here is our Steam link: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Forgetting the Past
item.description:
https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn8x195dnlro ""Seeing a concentration camp with my own eyes and listening to a survivor who went through it all, that's really brought it home. It's important for young people like me. We'll soon be able to vote. The far right is gaining more and more support in Germany and we need to learn from the past." [...] Eighteen-year-old Melike admitted she didn't know much about the Holocaust before coming to Dachau. Listening to Eva Umlauf, a survivor, talk about what happened, touched her heart, she said."
https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/holocaust-anniversary-1.7442237 "[...] A ceremony that is widely being treated as the last major observance that any notable number of survivors will be able to attend."
https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/elon-musks-call-germany-move-nazi-guilt-dangerous-holocaust-memorial-c-rcna189316 "“There is too much focus on past guilt, and we need to move beyond that,” he [Musk] added at the rally in the eastern German city of Halle." ... Hmm, it's almost like this guy is *encouraging* folks to forget about their history. How odd that this awkward-gesture-making feller would do that... Concerning!
item.dc:creator: ParryLost

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1255 (inch forward)
item.description:
Let us grind the bones of the rich to make our bread.
Cruel and unusual geography here.
Spite and whimsy, friends.
It doesn't have to be blackberries.
Don't be afraid to fight for your survival in your small space.
Visited mom in the hospital yesterday. She does not look good.
Yuuuuuup.
Scratch Scratch scratch
We do not live in vain.
I'm honestly surprised I haven't heard of any ICE raids here yet.
Pippin tax! No message from Pippin today, I spent the night in Phoenix. Messages resume tomorrow.
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Brooke Harrington, economical sociologist, muses with Jon Stewart on the Broligarchy
item.description:
She started in wealth management. Which is only for the 1%ers
After 17 years of studying stealth wealth, she wrote a book on what wealthy economics looks like behind the scenes Sub Rosa - under the rose, done in secret
Karl Marx said the most dangerous group is an educated proletariat. He also said wide social change is possible when class conscious proletariat work together with class conscious bourgeoisie.
How interested would imgur be in social science communication content? Application, understanding, explanations, studies of the sciences of cooperation and conflict? https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=DvCmBOenfsQ someone make a hyperlink lol
item.dc:creator: StellaMatutina

item.title: LOL, apocalypse-proof!
item.description: Don't know what's funnier... him still owing $171k on what was a $130k vehicle at the time, or expecting insurance to cover his "broker fee". Or maybe it's the indestructible vehicle being totaled by an e-scooter.

item.dc:creator: GeofrontTmP

item.title: Auschwitz was unimaginable. Treblinka was even worse.
item.description: It was the second-deadliest camp behind Auschwitz, "only" 900,000 humans died there…but unlike Auschwitz, there was no work done there. It was literally a place created to kill. Auschwitz was 350+ acres, with all sorts of slave workshops. Treblinka was at most 42 acres. The only facilities were to kill, and burn. No work, no slavery, only death. Shooting fields and crematoriums. The horror of mankind is truly unlimited.
item.dc:creator: PballQhead

item.title: I made George Takei laugh - I win the Internet today.
item.description: MV edit: thanks very much for the updoots. If you need a mental break from everything, may I recommend the emergency reality shelter? https://inkican.com/emergency-reality-shelter
item.dc:creator: inkican

item.title: Never forget
item.description: 80 years ago Soviet troops of 60th Army of the First Ukrainian Front entered what was left of the Auschwitz concentration, labour, and extermination camp complex. What was left because days before the retreating Nazi staff made sure to destroy the crematoria and force approx. 60 thousand people to march West in the freezing cold, starving, and sick in what would become known as "death march". One of many happening to prisoners held in the vast network of camps distrubuted across occupied Europe. Left were 7000 people, mostly Jews, who survived a place where approx. 1 million of people perished between 1940-1945. Although not the first liberated Auschwitz became a symbol of horror that Nazi Germany unleashed upon Earth. Unlike many places discovered and documented across Europe by Western allies, Auschwitz, initially a concentration camp for Polish political prisoners (Auschwitz I) was transformed into a death factory (Auschwitz-Birkenau), a place where people were transported from across Europe only to be exterminated hours after their trains arrived. Mostly Jews of all nations but also Poles, Roma/Sinti/Gypsies, and many other social groups including priests, Jehovah's witnesses, homosexuals or POW's like Soviets. Some of these groups refer to Holocaust in their own languages with names like Shoah, Porajmos, or Zagłada corresponding to the trauma of losing entire societies to the Nazi killing machine. The times are dark, the political inclination toward rule of power and contempt for human rights is why you should spend a minute or two today read about the Holocaust and why this cannot happen again: https://www.theholocaustexplained.org/ https://www.auschwitz.org/ https://encyclopedia.ushmm.org/content/en/article/introduction-to-the-holocaust https://www.yadvashem.org/holocaust/about.html https://www.google.com/amp/s/www.bbc.co.uk/newsround/16690175.amp --- Did not expect it to reach FP. Thank you all. If you feel like it, you can donate to the Auschwitz museum (https://www.auschwitz.org/en/donate/) or at least follow them on Bluesky or Mastodon https://bsky.app/profile/auschwitzmemorial.bsky.social https://mastodon.world/@auschwitzmuseum They post daily, and last I looked maintaining the museum is a never-ending task.
item.dc:creator: howthehelldidwegethere

item.title: Memes vol 478

This is such a me meme- a MEme if you will
I asked my boyfriend the other day “how does it feel to be dating the person with the most perfect music taste in all of existence?” and he laughed in my face because he doesn’t like musicals but the man likes Travis Scott who’s favorite line is “it’s lit!” So like…
Ooohhh I should go make Cheetos Mac and cheese
